message:
fix(eprocess): reject NaN/Inf in config validation (bd-38lh0)

IEEE 754 NaN comparisons always return false, so range checks like
'p0 <= 0.0 || p0 >= 1.0' silently accepted NaN values. Add explicit
is_finite() guards for p0, lambda, and alpha before range checks.
Also upgrade constructor validation from debug_assert to assert so
invalid configs are caught in release builds.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
